title: The moon base has a hardware plan. It needs a software strategy, too.
summary: A moon base project has a hardware plan but lacks a software strategy. The article discusses the importance of software in space missions, drawing from the author's experience at SpaceX.

title: Seagate Space Signs MOU with Firefly Aerospace to Collaborate on Offshore Launch Infrastructure for Alpha
summary: Seagate Space Corporation and Firefly Aerospace have signed an MOU to collaborate on developing offshore launch infrastructure for the Alpha rocket. The partnership aims to enhance launch capabilities and support space missions.

title: Military space programs confront hidden supply constraints
summary: Military space programs face hidden supply chain constraints, with critical components like optical links and valves potentially becoming bottlenecks. An industry report highlights these risks as potential chokepoints for program execution.

title: Artemis 2 ready to fly around the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 mission is scheduled to fly around the moon on April 6, aiming to set a distance record while astronauts study part of the lunar farside.

title: Isaacman defends NASA budget proposal despite steep cuts
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman defended a proposed 25% budget cut for the agency in the fiscal year 2027 budget proposal. The cuts have sparked significant debate within the space industry.

title: NASA stops work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2
summary: NASA has halted work on a second mobile launch platform intended for an upgraded version of the Space Launch System the agency no longer plans to develop. The decision reflects changes in NASA's SLS upgrade planning.

title: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby
summary: NASA is finalizing science plans for the Artemis 2 lunar flyby mission, where astronauts will conduct observations during their lunar flyby. The mission is a key step toward future lunar exploration.

title: Atlas 5 launches Amazon Leo satellites
summary: An Atlas 5 rocket successfully launched a new batch of satellites for Amazon's broadband constellation on April 4. The mission aims to speed up the deployment of Amazon's LEO satellite network.

title: Space Force budget would more than double in Trump’s $1.5 trillion defense plan
summary: The U.S. Space Force's budget would more than double to $71 billion in fiscal year 2027 under Trump's proposed $1.5 trillion defense plan, with over $60 billion allocated for procurement, research, and development.

title: Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues after its engine burn towards the moon. The mission remains on track after its initial propulsion phase.

title: Astronaut Victor Glover is the latest in a long line of Black American explorers − including York, the enslaved man who played a key role in the Lewis and Clark expedition
summary: Astronaut Victor Glover is celebrated as part of a long history of Black American explorers, including York, an enslaved man who contributed to the Lewis and Clark expedition. His achievement highlights an underappreciated legacy in space exploration.

title: NASA Artemis 2 astronauts to make historic moon flyby today. Here's what to expect hour by hour (timeline)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are set to make a historic moon flyby today, with a detailed hour-by-hour timeline of the event. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Humanity is back at the moon! Artemis 2 astronauts arrive in lunar space
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts have reached the moon's sphere of influence, marking humanity's return to lunar space. The four crew members entered this region early Monday morning (April 6).

title: When the moon hits your eye from your Orion ship up high, that's a 'mare' for Artemis 2
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts recently observed the moon from their Orion spacecraft, expressing immense excitement. This milestone highlights preparations for the upcoming Artemis 2 mission.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts are about to see one of the rarest skywatching sights of all — a solar eclipse from beyond the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts will observe a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon's far side on April 6, conducting scientific observations during the event.

title: The breakout star of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission isn't an astronaut — it's the space toilet
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission features a newly developed space toilet, which is a standout innovation despite not being an astronaut. The toilet enables astronauts to perform bodily functions in lunar conditions.

title: The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more'
summary: The Rubin Observatory has discovered 11,000 new asteroids and measured tens of thousands more, offering a preview of the expected discovery surge once full operations begin.

title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby
summary: The Artemis 2 crew plans to recreate Apollo 8's iconic 'Earthrise' photo during an April 6 flyby. This mission aims to honor the historic 1968 image of Earth rising over the moon's horizon.

title: This is the inspiring Easter message an Artemis 2 astronaut has for Earth while traveling to the moon (video)
summary: NASA astronaut Victor Glover delivered an impromptu Easter message from the Artemis 2 Orion capsule during its journey toward the moon. The historic mission marks a significant step in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Dozens of hidden star streams found in the outskirts of our Milky Way galaxy
summary: Astronomers discovered dozens of hidden star streams in the outskirts of the Milky Way galaxy using Gaia data, providing new insights into galaxy formation and dark matter.

title: Thinking of You, Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, set for April 4, 2026, will feature astronaut Reid Wiseman looking back at Earth from the Orion spacecraft during its journey to the Moon. The crew includes NASA astronauts Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: NASA Answers Your Most Pressing Artemis II Questions
summary: NASA has updated details about the Artemis II mission, a crewed test flight around the Moon. The first crewed test flight under NASA's Artemis program is currently underway with four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft.

title: Illuminated in Orion
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen are seen inside the Orion crew capsule in a 2026 photo, with lights turned off to reduce glare.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch
summary: NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting April 8 for the CRS-24 resupply mission to the ISS. The Cygnus XL spacecraft, carrying 11,000 pounds of cargo, will launch aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Meet NASA’s New Artemis II Science Officers
summary: NASA has appointed its first Artemis II science officers—Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia—to integrate advanced lunar science into Mission Control. Their roles will enhance how future Artemis crews study and understand the Moon.

title: Virgil I. “Gus” Grissom
summary: The news commemorates the 100th anniversary of Virgil I. 'Gus' Grissom's birth, highlighting his role as one of NASA's first seven astronauts and America's second astronaut to fly in space aboard the Liberty Bell 7 in 1961.

title: Hello, World
summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, commander of Artemis II, captured a stunning image of Earth from the Orion spacecraft showing two auroras and zodiacal light. This is one of the first photos taken after the translunar injection burn during the mission.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Leaves Earth Orbit for Flight around Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully executed a translunar injection burn, marking the first time astronauts have been bound for a lunar flyby in over 50 years. The six-minute engine firing propelled the Orion spacecraft out of Earth's orbit toward the Moon.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission will launch four astronauts on a 10-day journey around the Moon in April 2026. The crew includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in a critical phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone in the mission's journey toward the Moon.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads to the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening At 23:35 UTC on Dec. 16, 1972, the Apollo 17 command module, America, success

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in space exploration. The company's involvement highlights advancements in rocket technology for crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California for the Transporter 16 mission. This marks a significant milestone in the company's rideshare program.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Ottobrunn, Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company is part of the growing private space sector in Europe.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch date. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders revealed the plans.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is rapidly advancing construction on Starbase launch pads to support the upcoming Block 3 Starship configuration, aiming to increase launch cadence. Engineers are actively working to prepare the infrastructure for the next generation of Starship vehicles.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development without specifying details.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating its 50-year history, highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the agency's contributions to space exploration and science.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed with versatile capabilities, potentially revolutionizing multiple applications in the space industry. The innovation highlights advancements in semiconductor technology for aerospace use.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the recovery of the spacecraft.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, aimed at discovering Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: The State of Satcom 2026
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are disrupting the satcom market, forcing traditional players and startups to pivot and find innovative strategies to compete.

title: Artemis II Supplier Series: Orion’s Windows
summary: The article discusses the importance of Orion's windows for astronauts on the Artemis II mission, highlighting the significance of seeing space firsthand after years of training. It is part of a series focusing on suppliers for the Artemis II mission.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, maintaining the same funding level as the 2026 request. The proposal includes cuts to NASA's science and STEM missions.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper' during an industry seminar. The article highlights the difficulties faced in this emerging sector.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and statistics defining the space industry in Q1 2026, with a focus on the resurgence of Earth Observation (EO) capabilities. It highlights the growth and advancements in this sector during the first quarter of 2026.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed lunar landings starting next year, which has excited CLPS companies. The initiative aims to support sustained lunar exploration and development.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with four astronauts, including the first woman and person of color to head to the Moon. The crew also includes the first non-American astronaut to participate in a lunar mission.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding. The company aims to build an uncrewed space station to host manufacturing infrastructure in orbit.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has raised $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with participation from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other investors.

title: Live in the Booth: Matt Desch Talks Iridium NTN Direct and Alt PNT Advances
summary: Iridium is launching Iridium NTN Direct, a standardized and global narrow-band satellite IoT service, with CEO Matt Desch highlighting its significance. This initiative positions Iridium as the first in this space.

title: Redwire Wins European Quantum Satellite Contract
summary: Redwire has won a contract to develop a quantum-secure satellite for the European Space Agency's Quantum Key Distribution Satellite (QKDSat) program.

title: African Market for Satellite Services Offers Pent Up Demand
summary: Africa's large landmass has historically made satellite service deployment challenging, but demand for these services is growing rapidly as of September 2025.

title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Globalstar, According to FT Report
summary: Amazon is in discussions to potentially acquire Globalstar, according to a Financial Times report. The talks were first reported by FT citing unnamed sources.

title: Gravitics Receives Strategic Funding Increase From SpaceWERX
summary: Gravitics secured a Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract from SpaceWERX to accelerate the development of its Orbital Carrier architecture. The funding aims to support the U.S. Space Force's innovation goals.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate on a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing. The partnership aims to develop a capability for safely removing satellites from orbit.

title: In-Orbit Logistics Companies Call for Clearer Demand Signals from Gov
summary: In-orbit logistics companies urge the government to provide clearer demand signals. The Space Development Agency highlights the challenges of operating in space.

title: Canada’s Northwestel to Provide Broadband With Telesat Lightspeed Capacity
summary: Canada's Northwestel has signed a multi-year contract with Telesat to use capacity from its Lightspeed constellation for broadband services. This agreement will help Northwestel provide connectivity in remote regions like Nunavut and the Northwest Territories.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Component Supplier Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space Corporation has acquired Thermal Management Technologies (TMT) to support its orbital data center constellation development and vertical integration strategy.

title: ArkEdge Space to Work With ABIT For IoT Service Rollout
summary: ArkEdge Space and ABIT Corporation have signed an MoU to jointly develop IoT solutions and satellite communication devices. The partnership aims to enhance satellite-based IoT services.
